message:
chore(beads): update issue tracker state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -79,7 +79,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1s7","title":"Verification matrix schema v1: evidence row contract + required fields","description":"Deliverables:
- Define canonical row schema for each bead: unit_cmds, e2e_cmds, expected_assertions, log_schema_refs, artifact_paths, perf_proof_refs.
- Define status states: missing | partial | complete with deterministic transitions.

Acceptance:
- Schema is machine-readable and versioned.
- At least one concrete filled row example for each stream (docs/e2e/syscall/stubs/math/perf).

Proposed Row Template (v1):
```json
{
  "bead_id": "bd-XXXX",
  "stream": "docs|e2e|syscall|stubs|hard-parts|math|perf",
  "status": "missing|partial|complete",
  "unit": {
    "required": true,
    "commands": ["..."],
    "assertions": ["..."],
    "artifacts": ["..."]
  },
  "e2e": {
    "required": true,
    "modes": ["strict", "hardened"],
    "commands": ["..."],
    "artifacts": ["..."]
  },
  "logging": {
    "required": true,
    "schema_version": "v1",
    "required_fields": ["trace_id","mode","api_or_symbol","outcome","errno","timing_ns","artifact_refs"],
    "artifacts": ["..."]
  },
  "perf_proof": {
    "required": false,
    "baseline_cmds": ["..."],
    "profile_cmds": ["..."],
    "isomorphism_refs": ["..."]
  },
  "close_blockers": ["missing_unit","missing_e2e","missing_logs","missing_perf_proof"],
  "notes": "..."
}
```

Definition of Complete Row:
- `status=complete` only if all required sections have at least one command and one artifact reference.
- Any missing required field keeps `status` at `missing` or `partial` and must block closure.","notes":"Implemented row-schema contract v1 in tests/conformance/verification_matrix.json with canonical fields (unit_cmds, e2e_cmds, expected_assertions, log_schema_refs, artifact_paths, perf_proof_refs), deterministic status states/transitions (missing|partial|complete), and concrete filled stream examples for docs/e2e/syscall/stubs/math/perf. Enforced in scripts/check_verification_matrix.sh and crates/glibc-rs-harness/tests/verification_matrix_test.rs. Validation: scripts/check_verification_matrix.sh; cargo test -p glibc-rs-harness --test verification_matrix_test.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhiteMeadow","created_at":"2026-02-11T05:52:32.838216538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:35:32.626565604Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T06:35:32.626547681Z","close_reason":"Added row schema v1 contract with deterministic transitions and per-stream examples; gate + test enforcement added","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","logging","testing","verification"]}
 {"id":"bd-1sp","title":"Epic: Alien CS Graveyard Integration for FrankenLibC","description":"Integration of highest-impact alien CS concepts: Separation Logic proofs for TSM (section 5.8), HTM fast-path for hot libc functions (section 4.2), Liquid Types for compile-time bounds checking (section 5.5), CHERI capability integration (section 3.7), RCU/QSBR for hot metadata (section 14.8), Flat Combining for contended state (section 14.2), Seqlocks for config hot paths (section 14.9), EBR for lock-free structures (section 14.10), PCC at FFI boundary (section 11.9), Property-based testing with shrinking (section 6.12).

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:58:59.056702175Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:07:26.401338288Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-cs","cross-cutting","epic","frankenlibc","graveyard"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1sp","depends_on_id":"bd-2x5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1sp","depends_on_id":"bd-32e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":694,"issue_id":"bd-1sp","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:05Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1sp.1","title":"Alien CS: RCU/QSBR for thread-local hot metadata (section 14.8)","description":"Implement RCU (Read-Copy-Update) with QSBR (Quiescent-State-Based Reclamation) for hot thread-local metadata reads. Zero-overhead reads for thread-local state. Covers TLS cache, arena metadata, and configuration hot paths. Must integrate with existing TSM validation pipeline.

## Acceptance Criteria

Workspace check/clippy clean. See bead notes for full details.","created_at":"2026-03-12T06:11:51Z"}]}

Target: malloc metadata, stdio FILE* locks, pthread mutex operations. Must demonstrate measurable scaling improvement under >4 thread contention.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

@@ -348,7 +348,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-3h1u.3","title":"Property-based testing for all ABI families (proptest + cargo-fuzz)","description":"Implement comprehensive property-based testing using proptest for structured property testing and cargo-fuzz for coverage-guided fuzzing, covering all ABI families. For each ABI family, define properties that must hold: (1) String family — strlen(s) == manual_count(s), strcmp(a,b) == -strcmp(b,a), strcat output contains both inputs, memcpy(dst,src,n) makes dst[0..n] == src[0..n]. (2) Stdlib family — malloc(n) returns aligned pointer or NULL, free(malloc(n)) does not crash, realloc preserves existing data. (3) Math family — sin/cos within ULP bounds of reference, monotonicity where expected, NaN propagation. (4) Stdio family — fprintf/fscanf round-trip preserves values, fread/fwrite round-trip preserves bytes. (5) Thread family — mutex lock/unlock is paired, condvar signal wakes exactly one waiter. Implementation: proptest strategies for each input type (arbitrary strings, valid/invalid pointers, file descriptors). cargo-fuzz targets for each family with ASan/MSan/TSan instrumentation. CI integration: run proptest with 10K cases per property on each PR, run cargo-fuzz for 5 minutes per target nightly. Crash artifacts stored with full reproduction instructions.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Property-based testing from Claessen & Hughes (2000) QuickCheck. Coverage-guided fuzzing from Zalewski (2014) AFL. Rust tooling: proptest crate, cargo-fuzz with libFuzzer.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- Property definitions: one proptest Strategy per ABI family (e.g., arb_string(), arb_size(), arb_float()).
- proptest! macro for each property: e.g., proptest!(|(s in arb_string())| assert_eq!(strlen(s.as_ptr()), s.len())).
- cargo-fuzz targets: one per family in fuzz/fuzz_targets/. Instrumented with ASan + MSan.
- CI: proptest runs 10K cases per property on each PR. cargo-fuzz runs 5 minutes per target nightly.
- Crash artifact storage: fuzz/artifacts/ with full reproduction command in each artifact directory.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. Properties defined for all 6 major ABI families (string, stdlib, math, stdio, thread, memory).
2. At least 30 properties total across all families.
3. proptest runs 10K cases per property with zero failures on current implementation.
4. cargo-fuzz targets for string, allocator, printf families with at least 1 hour total fuzzing without crashes.
5. Any crash artifact includes reproduction command that triggers the exact same failure.
6. ASan/MSan/TSan instrumentation active during fuzzing (no silent memory errors).
7. Property violation produces minimal failing input via proptest shrinking.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- proptest: CI reports property_name, cases_run, time_ms, result, shrunk_counterexample (if failed).
- cargo-fuzz: CI reports target, corpus_size, executions, coverage_pct, crashes_found.
- Crash artifacts: fuzz_crash_report.json with target, input_hex, stack_trace, sanitizer_report.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MagentaFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:11.824328945Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T07:27:15.280502962Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3h1u.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2tq.3","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3h1u.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3h1u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":990,"issue_id":"bd-3h1u.3","author":"MaroonMoose","text":"2026-02-15 progress (MaroonMoose): Added proptest-based property coverage across six core ABI family surfaces in frankenlibc-core: string/mem, string/str, stdlib/conversion, math/trig, stdio/buffer, pthread/mutex. Added dev-dependency proptest=1.6 in crates/frankenlibc-core/Cargo.toml.\n\nNew property tests added: 18 total (3 per touched family module), including antisymmetry/invariants for compare ops, prefix mutation invariants for memory/buffer ops, numeric round-trip properties, invalid-base rejection semantics, and trig symmetry/round-trip properties.\n\nValidation run with RCH offload:\n- rch exec -- cargo fmt --check -p frankenlibc-core (pass)\n- rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-core --all-targets (pass)\n- rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-core --all-targets -- -D warnings (pass)\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core prop_ -- --nocapture (pass: 18 property tests)\n\nWorkspace-wide notes:\n- rch exec -- cargo fmt --check fails in unrelated pre-existing files under frankenlibc-abi formatting drift.\n- rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets surfaced unrelated existing failures/hangs in broader suites (e.g., rcu tests and long pthread thread test), outside this bead edit surface.\n\nBead remains in_progress; next slice should extend properties into remaining families and connect with fuzz target evidence/log artifacts.","created_at":"2026-02-15T23:57:54Z"},{"id":991,"issue_id":"bd-3h1u.3","author":"MagentaFalcon","text":"2026-02-25 progress (MagentaFalcon): advanced property-test coverage to 30 passing proptests in frankenlibc-core.\n\nCode updates in this slice:\n- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/mutex.rs\n  - added prop_init_from_reinit_states_transitions_to_unlocked (valid mutex kinds + {Uninitialized, Destroyed} + Init => Unlocked, errno=0, non-blocking).\n- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdio/buffer.rs already contains prop_set_mode_before_io_resets_state and participated in passing run (no new diff in this slice).\n\nValidation (all cargo-heavy commands via rch):\n- rch exec -- cargo fmt --package frankenlibc-core\n- rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-core --all-targets\n- rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-core --all-targets -- -D warnings\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core prop_ -- --nocapture\n\nResults:\n- check: pass\n- clippy: pass\n- prop tests: pass (30 passed, 0 failed)\n\nNotes:\n- rch worker repo-updater pull failed transiently on remote workers, but fallback workspace sync completed and commands executed successfully.\n- Broader bead scope (fuzz-target coverage + sanitizer-hour evidence + CI/nightly wiring) remains open; this is an incremental closure toward AC #2/#3.","created_at":"2026-02-25T07:27:15Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3h1u.4","title":"CI artifact gates","description":"Implement CI artifact gates that enforce quality thresholds before any merge to main. Gates: (1) Conformance gate — differential conformance matrix must not regress (no new failures vs previous release). (2) Performance gate — no symbol may exceed 110% of its baseline latency at p99 (10% regression budget). (3) Safety gate — all SOS certificates must evaluate correctly (SHA-256 hash verification + spot-check evaluation). (4) Coverage gate — line coverage must not decrease, branch coverage must not decrease by more than 0.5%. (5) Proof gate — all formal proof artifacts must be present and internally consistent (checksums match). (6) Evidence gate — evidence ledger must record all safety-critical decisions made during test runs. (7) Size gate — binary size must not exceed 110% of baseline (prevent bloat). Each gate produces a structured pass/fail report with details on what failed and why. All gates must pass for merge. Emergency override requires two approvals and creates an audit trail entry. Gate results are archived as release artifacts for traceability.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** CI quality gates from Humble & Farley (2010) Continuous Delivery. Gate design from Google SRE (2016) release engineering.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- GateRunner: CI script that evaluates all gates and produces structured pass/fail report.
- Each gate implemented as independent check with JSON output: { gate: name, result: pass|fail, details: ... }.
- Emergency override: requires --emergency-override flag + 2 approver names in commit message.
- Gate results archived as GitHub Actions artifacts with retention = 90 days.
- Binary size gate: compare stripped binary size against baseline stored in .beads/baselines/binary_size.json.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. All 7 gates implemented and running in CI pipeline.
2. Gate failure blocks merge (enforced via GitHub branch protection).
3. Emergency override creates audit trail entry in evidence ledger.
4. Gate reports parseable by CI dashboard (JSON schema validated).
5. All gates complete in <10 minutes total (parallel execution where possible).
6. False positive rate <1% (gates should not block legitimate changes).
7. Gate results accessible via gh run view for post-hoc analysis.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- Per gate: gate_name, result, duration_ms, details written to ci_gate_report.json.
- Override events: tracing::warn!(target: ci_gates, override, gate, approvers, justification).
- Historical: gate_history.json tracking pass/fail trend over last 50 runs.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:23.085524471Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:07:38.586351771Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3h1u.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3h1u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3h1u.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3h1u.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3h1u.5","title":"Decision Contract for strict/hardened mode (safe/suspicious/unsafe states)","description":"Define and implement the formal decision contract governing strict/hardened mode transitions and the safe/suspicious/unsafe state machine. The contract specifies: (1) State definitions — Safe: all TSM checks pass, no anomalies detected. Suspicious: one or more soft anomalies (e.g., unusual allocation pattern, pointer near boundary). Unsafe: hard violation detected (UAF, double-free, buffer overflow). (2) Transition rules — Safe->Suspicious: any soft anomaly. Suspicious->Unsafe: hard violation OR timeout in suspicious state. Suspicious->Safe: all checks pass for N consecutive operations. Unsafe->Safe: only via explicit repair in hardened mode. (3) Mode behavior — Strict: observe and log all transitions but never modify behavior. Report violations via evidence ledger. Hardened: actively intervene at Suspicious (increase monitoring) and Unsafe (quarantine + repair). (4) Contract enforcement — the decision contract is a state machine encoded as a const array of (state, event, next_state, action) tuples. The TSM evaluates this table on every operation. (5) Verification — the contract must satisfy: no deadlocks (from every state, there exists a path to Safe), no unhandled events (every state x event combination has a defined transition), and monotonic safety (once Unsafe, cannot return to Safe without explicit repair).

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** State machine specification from Harel (1987) Statecharts. Decision tables from Hurley (1983). Mode-based safety from Leveson (2004) STAMP.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- DecisionContract: const array of (TsmState, Event, TsmState, Action) tuples.
- TsmState enum { Safe, Suspicious, Unsafe }. Event enum { SoftAnomaly, HardViolation, CheckPass, RepairComplete, Timeout }.
- Action enum { Log, IncrMonitor, Quarantine, Repair, Escalate, ClearSuspicion }.
- Contract evaluation: O(1) lookup via 2D array indexed by [state][event].
- Verification: compile-time checks via const fn verifying no deadlocks, no unhandled events, monotonic safety.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. Decision contract covers all state x event combinations (3 states x 5 events = 15 entries).
2. No deadlock: from every state, exists path to Safe (verified by const fn at compile time).
3. No unhandled event: every state x event has defined transition (exhaustive match).
4. Monotonic safety: Unsafe -> Safe requires explicit RepairComplete event (never automatic).
5. Contract evaluation <5ns (single array lookup, no branching).
6. Integration test: inject sequence of events, verify state transitions match contract.
7. Contract visualization: state machine diagram generated from contract data (docs/tsm_state_machine.svg).

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- tracing::info!(target: decision_contract, from_state, event, to_state, action) on every transition.
- tracing::warn!(target: decision_contract, escalation, from_state, event) on Escalate action.
- State machine diagram auto-generated from contract data by CI script.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DustyCove","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:33.979059579Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T03:19:19.492392081Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3h1u.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3h1u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":992,"issue_id":"bd-3h1u.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (BlackForest): implemented decision-contract state machine + runtime policy integration.\n\nCode changes:\n- Added new module  with exhaustive 3x5 transition table ( x ).\n- Added compile-time contract invariants: exhaustive coverage, unsafe monotonic gate ( only via ), and path-to-safe reachability checks.\n- Added runtime tracker  with configurable suspicious clear threshold (default 3 consecutive ).\n- Exported contract types in .\n- Integrated contract tracking into ABI runtime policy (): decisions now map to contract events and store , , and  in .\n- Added ABI tests covering strict-mode log projection and hardened repair completion edge.\n\nValidation commands (all offloaded with ):\n-

 {"id":"bd-3hud","title":"bd-yos subtask: Join/detach lifecycle state machine and error semantics","description":"Background:
- join/detach lifecycle correctness is essential to avoid resource leaks and undefined ownership states.

Goal:
- Implement deterministic thread lifecycle state machine for join/detach/exit-value propagation.

Deliverables:
1) Thread handle lifecycle states.
2) Join semantics with exit status delivery.
3) Detach semantics and resource reclamation.
4) Error handling (double-join, join-detached, invalid handle).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Lifecycle operations are race-safe and deterministic under test.
- Error codes and edge behavior are explicit and verified.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for lifecycle transitions and misuse cases.
- Structured logs: trace_id, handle_id, op, old_state, new_state, return_value_ref, errno.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:52.033022201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:24:05.221352645Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T09:24:05.221285439Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","implementation","pthread","testing","threading"],"comments":[{"id":997,"issue_id":"bd-3hud","author":"CrimsonForge","text":"COMPLETED: Join/detach lifecycle state machine and error semantics (bd-3hud).

Changes from bd-mjon baseline:

1. CAS-protected state transitions:
   - Trampoline: STARTING -> RUNNING via compare_exchange (prevents overwriting DETACHED)
   - Trampoline: RUNNING -> FINISHED via compare_exchange (detects DETACHED for self-cleanup)
   - join_thread: FINISHED -> JOINED via CAS (only one joiner wins)
   - detach_thread: RUNNING/STARTING -> DETACHED via CAS (only one detacher wins)
   - detach_thread: FINISHED -> DETACHED via CAS (races safely with concurrent joiner)

2. Self-join detection (EDEADLK):
   - join_thread checks if calling thread's TID matches target's TID

3. Detached thread self-cleanup:
   - Trampoline detects DETACHED state via CAS failure
   - Calls set_tid_address(NULL) to prevent kernel CLONE_CHILD_CLEARTID write to freed memory
   - Frees heap-allocated ThreadHandle
   - Stack reclaimed by OS on process exit (munmap deferred to bd-rth1 for signal safety)

4. Error semantics:
   - NULL handle: EINVAL (22) for both join and detach
   - Self-join: EDEADLK (35)
   - Join already-joined: EINVAL
   - Join detached: EINVAL
   - Detach already-joined: EINVAL
   - Detach already-detached: EINVAL

5. New syscall wrapper:
   - sys_set_tid_address(tidptr) in syscall/mod.rs

Files modified:
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/thread.rs: CAS state machine, self-cleanup, error codes
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/syscall/mod.rs: Added sys_set_tid_address

Quality gates: all PASS
Tests: 11/11 PASS (5 new for bd-3hud)","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:23:59Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3jzb","title":"BRAVO RW Locks for read-heavy paths (section 14.7)","description":"Implement BRAVO (Biased Reader And Visible Observer) reader-writer locks for read-heavy paths in FrankenLibC. BRAVO is a RW lock optimized for the common case of read-dominant workloads. Readers use a visible-reader protocol (write their TID to a shared slot on read entry, clear on exit). Writers check all slots for active readers. Key innovation: biased locking — the fast path for readers is a single atomic write (no CAS, no fence on x86-TSO). Writers pay the cost of scanning reader slots. Application to FrankenLibC: (1) Allocation metadata — the per-arena metadata (free list head, allocation count, generation counters) is read on every allocation for validation but written infrequently (only on actual alloc/free state changes). BRAVO eliminates reader contention. (2) TSM certificate cache — evaluated certificates are cached. Cache reads vastly outnumber writes (cache invalidation is rare). (3) Thread registry — the list of active threads is read by garbage collection and RCU grace period detection but modified only on thread creation/destruction. Performance target: reader fast path <5ns (single atomic store), writer path <100ns (slot scan + fence). Comparison: implement alongside RCU (bd-3aof) and Left-Right (bd-cs9w) — benchmark all three for FrankenLibC's specific read/write ratios to determine which is optimal for each use case.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.7 of graveyard. BRAVO from Dice, Kogan, & Herlihy (2019 USENIX ATC). Visible-reader protocol for biased RW locks optimized for read-dominant workloads.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- BravoRwLock<T>: wraps T with visible-reader protocol.
- Reader fast path: atomic store of TID to slot in reader table. Single store on x86-TSO (no fence needed).
- Reader table: #[repr(align(64))] array of AtomicU64 slots, sized to MAX_THREADS.
- Writer: acquire inner lock (Mutex), scan reader table for active readers, wait for all to clear.
- Slot assignment: hash(thread_id) mod TABLE_SIZE for O(1) slot lookup. Collision handling: linear probe.
- read() -> BravoReadGuard: write TID to slot, read data, clear slot on drop.
- write() -> BravoWriteGuard: acquire mutex, scan table, return mutable reference.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. BravoRwLock<T> compiles and passes miri for basic read/write cycle.
2. Reader fast path <5ns at p99 (single atomic store on x86-TSO).
3. Writer path <100ns at p99 including slot scan under 16-reader load.
4. 32-reader + 1-writer stress test: 1M operations, TSan clean.
5. Compare with RCU and Left-Right: benchmark all three on FrankenLibC metadata workload.
6. No reader starvation: readers always make progress regardless of writer activity.
7. Slot collision rate <5% at 64 threads (hash quality test).

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- tracing::trace!(target: bravo, operation=read, slot, reader_count) per read (disabled in release).
- tracing::debug!(target: bravo, operation=write, scan_time_ns, active_readers_found) per write.
- Benchmark comparison: bravo_vs_rcu_vs_leftright.json with throughput and latency per read/write ratio.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:29:46.141109287Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T18:20:46.762878982Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3jzb","depends_on_id":"bd-3aof","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
